summary: Replaced raw .incbin blobs in asm/MSL/abort_exit.s and the large asm/sdata2.s data section with explicit assembler data directives. The abort_exit .dtors table was expanded into named destructor pointers and NULL entries, while sdata2 labels were converted from baserom offsets into concrete .4byte constants, mostly float/double bit patterns. This was a cleanup PR with no review discussion in the provided slice.
what_changed: Removed the .incbin for the .dtors region at lbl_803B7260 in asm/MSL/abort_exit.s and replaced it with eight explicit .4byte entries.
Identified the abort_exit destructor table entries as __destroy_global_chain, __fini_cpp_exceptions, __destroy_global_chain, followed by five NULL words.
Converted many labels in asm/sdata2.s from .incbin "baserom.dol" ranges into explicit .4byte values.
Preserved the existing lbl_804Dxxxx global label structure in sdata2 while making the underlying bytes visible as constants.
The PR was large for a cleanup: 2 changed files, 7318 added lines, 5652 deleted lines, and a diff dominated by sdata2 data expansion.
smart_moves: Kept unknown or not-yet-renamed sdata2 symbols as address-style lbl_804Dxxxx labels rather than inventing semantic names unsupported by the diff.
Used exact word-level .4byte encodings for floating-point and double constants, preserving binary identity while removing dependence on baserom .incbin slices.
Resolved the small .dtors incbin into meaningful symbol references where the table contents were known, improving readability beyond merely dumping hex.
Made static data searchable by value and label, which helps future matching work connect compiler-generated constants to functions that reference them.
decomp_lessons: When removing incbins from data sections, prefer explicit assembler directives that reproduce bytes exactly; .4byte hex is safe for float/double bit patterns when semantic typing is not yet established.
For destructor or initializer arrays, named function-pointer entries are more useful than raw hex if the referenced symbols are known.
Large sdata2 cleanups can be done without prematurely assigning semantic names; maintaining stable lbl_804Dxxxx labels avoids misleading future contributors.
Replacing incbins with explicit constants improves grepability and makes later decompilation easier because constants can be correlated with generated code and relocation references.
NULL table entries should be represented explicitly as NULL where the assembler/context supports it, as shown in abort_exit.s, rather than opaque zero words.
naming_conventions: Unidentified small-data symbols remained in the standard address-derived form lbl_804D79E0, lbl_804D7A00, etc.
The .dtors table label remained lbl_803B7260 while entries referenced known MSL/runtime symbols such as __destroy_global_chain and __fini_cpp_exceptions.
No evidence in the slice of new semantic names being introduced for sdata2 constants.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replace .incbin "baserom.dol", offset, size with one or more .4byte directives whose total byte count matches the original size.
Represent 8-byte constants as two consecutive .4byte values, preserving big-endian word order as shown by constants like 0x3FF921FB / 0x54442D18.
For function-pointer tables, emit symbol references with .4byte rather than numeric addresses when target symbols are known.
Use explicit zero pairs for double zero or padding-sized 8-byte regions when the original incbin covered 0x8 bytes.
This PR appears to be data-materialization cleanup rather than C matching work; no function body matching tricks or control-flow tactics are evidenced.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
No reviewer rationale or requested changes are available; conclusions are based on title, PR body, changed files, and diff excerpt only.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename lbl_804Dxxxx sdata2 constants introduced or exposed by PR #94.
Search for additional incbin cleanup PRs in asm data sections and compare directive style.
Check whether later tooling converted any of these .4byte float/double bit patterns into .float or .double directives.
Trace references to lbl_803B7260, __destroy_global_chain, and __fini_cpp_exceptions in later MSL/runtime cleanup work.
